data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-9f20a4bd">
    <title>agent: verify claim heartbeat liveness stays out of beads.jsonl</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The original blocker was periodic claim liveness dirtying `.ddx/beads.jsonl` while `ddx work` was running. Current code appears to have the intended implementation, but the bead prompt is stale and still points at the old tracker-writing heartbeat path. Leaving the stale prompt open risks duplicate or regressive implementation work.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:884-897 now calls `w.Store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)` from the worker heartbeat goroutine instead of the old tracker-writing `Store.Heartbeat` path.
- cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:13-25 stores claim liveness under `$TMPDIR/ddx-claim-heartbeats/<project-hash>/<bead>.json`, outside `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:64-101 writes, removes, and reads the external lease file.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:660-685 keeps `Store.Heartbeat` only as a deprecated compatibility wrapper that validates status and then calls `TouchClaimHeartbeat`; it no longer writes heartbeat fields to the tracker.
- cli/internal/bead/store_test.go:775-791 already contains `TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go:64-96 already contains `TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL` and `TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Treat this bead as verification and closeout, not greenfield implementation.
- Confirm the work loop has no production periodic liveness call that writes `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Run the focused liveness/heartbeat tests and then the package gate used by worker beads.
- If a focused test fails, fix only the regression needed to keep liveness out of the tracker; do not introduce a second liveness store.
- If the focused tests and package gate pass, close the bead with the verifying commit/result.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign claim ownership, stale-claim TTL policy, worker watchdogs, or server worker state.
- Do not move durable semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, or evidence out of `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Do not implement Axon or another tracker backend in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. Existing dependency ddx-06eb05d8 is closed; no new deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL passes and proves heartbeat refresh does not change `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes.
2. TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker passes and proves the replacement claim liveness surface is readable outside the tracker.
3. TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite passes and proves the deprecated compatibility path does not write heartbeat data to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
4. A production-code search confirms `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` uses `TouchClaimHeartbeat` for periodic liveness and does not call `Store.Heartbeat` or write `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` during the heartbeat tick.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL|TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker|TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite|TestHeartbeatKeepsActiveClaimAlive|TestHeartbeatReclaimStaleInProgressBead" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, kind:bug, reliability, refactor, spec:FEAT-006, spec:FEAT-010, verification</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="3f729d3806820da2ad28af7be4981bdfee57f5ad" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T005605-e51867dc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Consumer of the Fizeau Execution Contract</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 7 acceptance criteria are satisfied with no code changes needed:

- **AC1–AC3**: All three focused tests pass (liveness tests + deprecated heartbeat test).
- **AC4**: `execute_bead_loop.go` uses `TouchClaimHeartbeat` at line 896; grep confirms zero references to `Store.Heartbeat` or `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` in that file.
- **AC5**: Focused `go test` run with all 5 filters — green.
- **AC6**: Full `./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` suite — green.
- **AC7**: `lefthook run pre-commit` — passes cleanly.

The implementation was already correct. `no_changes_rationale.txt` carries a `verification_command` so the loop can confirm by re-running the focused tests.
